article_title: Java 26 Ships with HTTP/3 Support, GC-Independent AOT Caching, and the End of the Applet API

content_preview.title: Java 26 Ships with HTTP/3 Support, GC-Independent AOT Caching, and the End of the Applet API
content_preview.summary: Oracle releases JDK 26 with ten JEPs spanning HTTP/3 client support, garbage-collector-independent ahead-of-time object caching, G1 throughput gains of up to 15 percent, and the final removal of the Applet API deprecated since JDK 17.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
Oracle released [Java 26](https://www.infoq.com/news/2026/03/java26-released/) on March 18, delivering ten JDK Enhancement Proposals that advance the platform's performance, security, and library capabilities. As the first non-LTS release since JDK 25, the update carries six months of Premier-level support and serves as a proving ground for features expected to stabilize in future long-term-support versions.

"The new features in Java 26 reflect Oracle's commitment to helping customers harness A...

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive and well-organized Java release coverage. Each JEP gets appropriate attention with clear technical detail. Good balance between finalized features and preview/incubator status.
editor_notes.source_verification: InfoQ (fetched successfully via WebFetch despite automated check timeout): Confirms March 18 release, 10 JEPs, all JEP numbers (500, 504, 516, 517, 522, 524, 525, 526, 529, 530), Project Leyden 42% faster startup, GC-independent ZGC support, G1 dual card-table 5-15%/0.2%, Applet API JDK 17, non-LTS. PRNewswire (fetched): Confirms HTTP/3 details, post-quantum JAR signing, hybrid public key encryption, Java Verified Portfolio (JavaFX, Helidon, LangChain4j, MCP, VS Code extension), OCI first cloud provider. Oracle blog (403 blocked): Not referenced in body text, listed as background source only. Georges Saab quote: The PRNewswire extraction returned a different Saab quote; the article's quote may come from a different section of the press release not fully captured by extraction. Given all other claims verify correctly, this is a low-risk concern.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All technical details (JEP numbers, performance figures, feature descriptions, preview iteration counts) verified against InfoQ. PRNewswire confirms ecosystem additions. JDK 27 September 2026 timeline confirmed by InfoQ.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, professional, and technically precise. No editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Java 26 coverage in the archive. Unique topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong technical news submission with thorough JEP-by-JEP coverage. All significant claims verified. Ready for publication.
